content:
When you enter the password, there should be a check box to remember the password. Are you using it?

What version of Thunderbird? What is your operating system? Do you use any add-ons that could affect this?

Do you have any other email accounts set up in Thunderbird? If so, their passwords should be listed as well. When you search, don't put anything in the Search box and make sure it's completely empty. Unless you have many accounts, the list should be short enough that you can just eyeball it.

You can try restarting Thunderbird in Troubleshoot Mode (found in the Help menu.) This will restart it with all add-ons temporarily disabled, in case one of them is misbehaving.

content:
Use Thunderbird's Import Tool feature.

- Restore your backed-up Profile to a convenient temporary location (Desktop?)

- Click on Thunderbird menu item <Tools | Import | Import from another Thunderbird installation>. Continue

- Choose one of the Profiles it had detected, or navigate to your backed-up Profile. Continue.

- Deselect everything but Calendars. Continue.

- Acknowledge the confirmation screen by clicking Start Import.

- When the import is complete, restart Thunderbird. If all looks OK, you may delete your temporary Profile copy.
creator: rednoise

content:
Reinstalling Thunderbird is not likely to help with things like this because all preferences and account information are stored in your Profile, which a reinstall will not touch.

What version of Thunderbird? What is your operating system? Do you, indeed, have a complete valid copy of your Profile?

Once we know what there is to work with there are actions you can take.

content:
I can't explain it but here's my experience...

I can unsubscribe from the Draft(s) folder on Gmail and Outlook.

I cannot unsubscribe from Draft(s) on Yahoo, Comcast, or a Roundcube webmail host. It behaves as you describe - you uncheck the box but it doesn't stick.I notice that on Roundcube you are able to Subscribe/Unsubscribe via sliders next to each folder. However, the sliders next to Inbox, Drafts, Sent, Junk, and Trash are grayed out and cannot be moved.

On Comcast web mail, the Inbox, Drafts, Sent, Spam, and Trash folders have no option to Delete, Rename, Move or any other operations that can be applied to your own folders.

My conclusion is that, on some servers, certain folders are intentionally configured to be protected and can't be changed by the user. Probably similar situation on other hosts, too.
creator: rednoise

content: I see that this is a common problem, but I do not see how this could be a *server* problem. My understanding is that the "subscription" toggle is just telling Thunderbird to not fetch the specific folder. In my specific case, there is really nothing special about the Draft and Sent folders, they are not even the Draft and Sent folder of the account, just two subfolders that happen to be called like that.

content: There's something else going on here. A fresh reply to an old message will show the current date when delivered, and it will be treated like any other new message: delivered to the Inbox unless diverted as spam or by message filters or rules. If you look at the message source, (Ctrl+U while the message is selected) you can see all the stops it made along the from the sender to you, with dates and time stamps.

content:
We would need to see any and all error messages you see, complete and verbatim to make more accurate suggestions. But the first thing you should do is confirm your email server settings, which may have changed during the migration. Here's Greenby's instructions page about that:

https://greenby.com/support/knowledgebase/article/29

For lack of information, I'm going to make some guesses...

If this is an IMAP account, the token (which is related to your password, but is not your password) may no longer be valid. You can start the authentication process fresh by deleting all current password/tokens for that account , which can be found at

<Settings | Privacy & Security | Passwords | Saved Passwords>.

Also, remove all cookies for that account. They can be found at:

<Settings | Privacy & Security | Web Content | Show Cookies>.

Restart Thunderbird and you should be prompted for your credentials, so have you user ID and password handy.

content:
Are you sure there is no "Subscribe" option in the context menu? You have to right-click on the account name, not the folder itself. And are you sure they are IMAP folders? There will be no Subscribe option for a POP3 account.

I did a short test just now:

- I added a new folder in Roundcube and copied a few messages into it. Then I looked at the Subscribe list and was surprised and pleased that the new folder showed up there and was automatically subscribed. However, the folder did not show up in the Folders pane until I collapsed and expanded the folders list. Re-starting Thunderbird does it, too.

- I renamed that folder in Roundcube. The folder acted similarly in Thunderbird as above - it was immediately visible with the new name in the Subscribe menu, but I had to do the collapse/expand thing to make it show up in the Folders list.

How does my experience compare with yours?

I notice that there are "Subscribe" buttons in Roundcube that accomplish the same thing as the Subscribe check boxes in Thunderbird.

Maybe it would help to unsubscribe from all folders, then re-subscribe, perhaps restarting Thunderbird in between.
creator: rednoise
